reasoning_content: We need to determine if 359 is prime. Check divisibility: 359 is odd, not divisible by 2. Sum of digits = 3+5+9=17, not divisible by 3. Last digit not 0 or 5, so not divisible by 5. Check 7: 7*51=357, remainder 2, so not divisible. 11: 11*32=352, remainder 7, no. 13: 13*27=351, remainder 8, no. 17: 17*21=357, remainder 2, no. 19: 19*18=342, remainder 17, no. 23: 23*15=345, remainder 14; 23*16=368 too high, so no. 29: 29*12=348, remainder 11; 29*13=377 too high. 31: 31*11=341, remainder 18; 31*12=372. 37: 37*9=333, remainder 26; 37*10=370. Next prime 41: 41*8=328, remainder 31; 41*9=369 too high. 43: 43*8=344, remainder 15; 43*9=387. 47: 47*7=329, remainder 30; 47*8=376. 53: 53*6=318, remainder 41; 53*7=371. 59: 59*6=354, remainder 5; 59*7=413. sqrt(359) is about 18.95, so we only need to check primes up to 19. Actually sqrt(359) ≈ 18.95, so primes up to 17 (since 19^2=361 > 359). We checked 2,3,5,7,11,13,17. None divide evenly. So 359 is prime. Answer: yes.
